truth.papers.A66.title: High Manganese and Aluminum Steels for the Military and Transportation Industry
truth.papers.A66.paper_scope: A review of tensile and high-strain-rate fracture properties of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C steels with silicon additions (0.59–1.56% Si) and varying phosphorus impurity levels, focusing on age hardening, notch toughness, and dynamic fracture toughness.

prediction.papers.A66.title: High Manganese and Aluminum Steels for the Military and Transportation Industry
prediction.papers.A66.paper_scope: Review of tensile and high-strain-rate fracture properties of austenitic Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C steels, focusing on the effects of silicon (0.59–1.56%) and impurity phosphorus (0.001–0.07%) on age-hardening response, microstructure evolution, and processing-structure-property relationships in cast and wrought conditions.

truth.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al09c.alloys_notes: Nominal base composition is Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C (wt%, unless otherwise specified). Silicon varied between 0.59 and 1.56% Si; phosphorus from 0.001 to 0.07% P; typical Mo content 0.3–0.5% Mo. Actual chemistries of six steels are provided in Table I.
truth.alloys.alloy_sae4130.alloys_notes: Benchmark alloy used for comparison in strength and dynamic fracture toughness.
truth.alloys.alloy_fe13mn12c_hadfield.alloys_n: Historical reference alloy from Hadfield, basis for high-Mn steels.

prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al09c.alloys_notes: Base composition for reviewed alloys. Fully austenitic when solution treated above 950-C. Density reduction up to 15% compared with quenched and tempered Cr-Mo steels.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si059.alloys_notes: Low-silicon, low-phosphorus variant from Table I.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si107_lp.alloys_notes: Low-phosphorus, 1.07% Si variant from Table I.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si156.alloys_notes: High-silicon, low-phosphorus variant from Table I.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si101.alloys_notes: Calcium-treated baseline variant from Table I.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si092_hp.alloys_notes: High-phosphorus variant from Table I. Table IV reports this as 0.94% Si, 0.043% P.
prediction.alloys.alloy_si107_hp.alloys_notes: High-phosphorus (0.07% P) variant from Table I and Table III.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe29mn6al.alloys_notes: Hot-rolled lightweight steel shown in Fig. 1b.
prediction.alloys.alloy_fe30mn9al27si.alloys_notes: High-silicon hot-rolled steel discussed in the notch toughness section.
prediction.alloys.alloy_sae4130.alloys_notes: Benchmark quenched and tempered Cr-Mo steel used for comparison.

truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_01.descripti: As-cast condition, no subsequent heat treatment mentioned beyond initial solidification.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_02.descripti: Solution treatment at 1050°C for 2 h followed by water quench.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_02.processes: Slow quenching after solution treatment is noted to decrease notch toughness due to heterogeneous precipitation of κ-carbide on grain boundaries.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_03.descripti: Solution treatment at 1050°C for 2 h, water quench, followed by aging at 530°C or 570°C for various durations.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_03.processes: Aging temperature range for κ-carbide precipitation is 450–700°C. Specific aging times: 10 h, 20 h, 22 h, 26 h, 30 h, 40 h, 60 h, 90 h at 530°C; 4 h, 10 h at 570°C.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_04.descripti: Hot rolled at 900°C to 84% reduction in thickness, solution treated for 10 min at 900°C or 1100°C, water quenched.
truth.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_04.processes: Wrought processing route. Some material slow cooled after hot rolling before solution treatment. Hot rolling refines grain size.

prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_01.descripti: As-cast condition; cast plates or ingots.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_01.processes: Cast steels have low melting points, less than 1350-C, and excellent filling characteristics.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_02.descripti: Solution treated at 1050-C for 2 h and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_02.processes: Produces fully austenitic matrix. Slow quenching can cause heterogeneous precipitation of k-carbide along grain boundaries.
prediction.processes.proc_st_900_10m_wq.description: Solution treated at 900-C for 10 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_st_900_10m_wq.processes_n: Used for wrought hot-rolled material to produce equiaxed grain structure.
prediction.processes.proc_st_1100_10m_wq.descriptio: Solution treated at 1100-C for 10 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_st_1100_10m_wq.processes_: Applied to hot-rolled high-silicon steel to improve toughness.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_03.processes: Primary age-hardening temperature. Precipitation of nanosized k-carbide. Times range from 10 h to 90 h depending on alloy.
prediction.processes.proc_aged_570_var.processes_no: Higher aging temperature used for some calcium-treated steels.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_04.descripti: Hot rolled at 900-C to an 84% reduction in thickness, then solution treated at 900-C for 10 min and water quenched.
prediction.processes.proc_fe30mn9al09c_04.processes: Wrought processing producing equiaxed austenite grains 10 lm to 20 lm in diameter.
prediction.processes.proc_hr_only.description: Hot rolled only, slow cooled after deformation.
prediction.processes.proc_hr_only.processes_notes: Results in SRO and precipitation of k-carbide due to slow cooling, leading to brittle behavior.
prediction.processes.proc_qt_4130.processes_notes: Benchmark heat treatment for SAE 4130 cast steel.

Standard solution treatment for cast steels to dissolve κ-carbide and homogenize austenite.
Aging leads to homogeneous coherent precipitation of nano-sized κ-carbide (Fe,Mn)3AlCx with E21 cubic perovskite structure. Peak aging hardness varies with Si and P content.
Applied to Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C wrought steel with 0.023% P.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_si059_st.process: Solution treated to produce fully austenitic matrix.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe29mn6al_hr.pro: Hot rolled to produce fine equiaxed austenite grains with annealing twins.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_fe30mn9al27si_hr: Hot rolled and slow cooled after deformation.
Solution treatment after hot rolling to dissolve k-carbide and improve toughness.
prediction.processing_steps.sample_wrought_101si_pe: Peak aged condition. Precipitation of k-carbide on grain boundaries.

truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al09c_01.overal: As-cast microstructure consisting of large primary austenite dendrites with grain structures on the order of millimeters.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al09c_04.overal: Equiaxed austenite grains with annealing twins, refined by hot rolling and recrystallization. Grain boundaries pinned by NbC in some variants.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al09c_04.micros: fine equiaxed grains with annealing twins
10–20 μm average diameter (Fig. 6), or average grain size of 50 μm for steel with NbC pinning (Fig. 1b)
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.overal: Fully austenitic matrix with homogeneously distributed coherent nano-sized κ-carbide precipitates after aging. Precipitation can also occur on grain boundaries after peak aging.
truth.structures.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.micros: increases with Si content and aging time
initially ordered domains (few unit cells), later cuboidal and plate morphologies arranged along <100>γ; cube-on-cube orientation relationship with matrix: <100>κ // <100>γ, {001}κ // {001}γ
planar slip bands due to κ-carbide shearing
increased slip band density with age hardening
constrained mismatch between κ and γ increases with Si content due to increased C partitioning
nanosized; ordered domains ~ few unit cells initially; up to ~5 nm average in 0.043% P steel aged 0.5 h at 570°C; larger with higher Si and P; dark-field TEM in Fig. 3 and Fig. 4 nm
homogeneous precipitation in austenite matrix; grain boundary precipitation after peak aging
coherent with austenite matrix; cube-on-cube orientation relationship; coherency stress increases with Si content

prediction.structures.sample_si092_hp_530_10h.overa: High-phosphorus aged microstructure with larger ordered domains
prediction.structures.sample_si092_hp_530_10h.micro: high number density, coarsening along elastically soft <100>

truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.defect: Shearing of coherent κ-carbide by dislocations leads to planar slip localization.
Planar slip bands; slip traces on {111} cleavage facets; dislocation accumulation on relatively few slip bands increases propensity for {111} glide plane decohesion, especially at high strain rates.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.interf: Carbon partitioning from γ to κ increases the γ-κ constrained mismatch, contributing to spinodal hardening. Silicon increases C activity in austenite, raising C partitioning coefficient from 2.1 (0.59%Si) to 2.9 (1.56%Si) after 60 h at 530°C.
Glide plane decohesion ("slipping off") producing {111}-type fracture facets. Phosphorus substitution for Al in κ-carbide reduces {100} cleavage stress by 45%.
truth.interfaces.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.phase_: Spinodal decomposition into C-rich or C-and-Al-rich/depleted zones precedes or is concurrent with short-range ordering (SRO) into E21 lattice sites; coarsening leads to cuboidal and plate morphologies. Silicon accelerates κ-carbide formation and increases volume fraction; phosphorus enhances SRO kinetics and spinodal decomposition rate.

prediction.interfaces.sample_si107_lp_530_60h.phase: Precipitation of k-carbide in the temperature range 450-700-C results from compositional modulation by spinodal decomposition. Silicon increases carbon partitioning coefficient from 2.1 to 2.9, increasing constrained mismatch and hardening.
prediction.interfaces.sample_si092_hp_530_10h.inter: Phosphorus substitution for aluminum in k-carbide relaxes local bonding along <100>, resulting in a 45% reduction in cleavage stress of k-carbide.
prediction.interfaces.sample_si092_hp_530_10h.phase: Phosphorus increases the prevalence of ordering reaction and accelerates spinodal decomposition during aging. P substitutes for Al corner positions in k-carbide, predicted to create open-volume defects on {100} under stress.

truth.properties.sample_fe30mn9al09c_02.mechan: High work-hardening rates in solution-treated condition.
truth.properties.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.mechan: Work-hardening ability reduced with age hardening.
truth.properties.sample_fe30mn9al09c_04.mechan: increased by 200 MPa with aging; peak aging tensile strength up to ~1085 MPa cast, wrought higher MPa

truth.performance.sample_fe30mn9al09c_03.compa: up to 15% density reduction; equivalent or higher dynamic fracture toughness (700 kJ/m² vs. 94–136 kJ/m²); tensile strength up to 1085 MPa comparable.

prediction.performance.sample_si156_530_10h.compara: Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.9C with 1.07% Si aged to 310 BHN

truth.characterization_methods.sample_fe30mn9a: optical microscopy (bright-field, differential interference contrast imaging)
As-cast Fe-28Mn-3Al-0.9Si-1.2C-0.6Mo steel shows large primary austenite dendrites. Etched with 10% nital.

Hot-rolled Fe-29Mn-6Al-0.9C-0.29Si-0.44Nb-0.44Mo steel: equiaxed austenite grains with annealing twins, average grain size 50 μm, pinned by NbC on grain boundaries. Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.01Si-0.9C-0.5Mo in peak aged condition shows preferential grain boundary etching due to κ-carbide and banding from segregation.

Transition from ductile tearing (0.001% P) to low-energy cleavage-like fracture producing dendritic pattern along {001} (0.07% P). Brittle transgranular cleavage with {111} slip traces on favorably oriented facets in aged/solution-treated specimens, indicating glide plane decohesion.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_si107_lp: Phase identification and lattice parameter measurement
No spinodal decomposition or SRO in solution-treated specimens regardless of Si content. Silicon decreases austenite lattice parameter during aging.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_si059_st: Microstructural imaging of as-cast and hot-rolled microstructures
As-cast alloys consist of large primary austenitic dendrites producing millimeter-scale grain structures.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_fe29mn6a: Hot-rolled steel consists of fine equiaxed austenite grains and annealing twins, average grain size 50 um, pinned by NbC on grain boundaries.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_si059_53: SEM with ASPEX automated feature analysis
High AlN inclusion density is deleterious to impact properties. Porosity in 0.59% Si steel was more than twice that of 1.07% Si steel.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Predicted yield strength increase of 120 MPa/wt.% Si for specimens aged 60 h at 530°C, in reasonable agreement with experimental mechanical properties. Predicted 45% reduction in {100} cleavage stress of κ-carbide due to P substitution for Al, consistent with observed notch toughness loss.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Explains increased C activity in austenite and enhanced C partitioning into κ-carbide due to repulsive Si-C interaction.
C partitioning coefficient from 2.1 (0.59%Si) to 2.9 (1.56%Si); austenite lattice parameter decrease; κ-carbide lattice parameter increase.
Silicon substitution in austenite increases repulsive Si-C interaction, forcing more carbon into κ-carbide.
Spinodal hardening mechanism (Kato et al. derived)
Predicts yield strength increase from spinodal decomposition modulated structure.
120 MPa/wt.% Si increase predicted for 60 h aging at 530°C.
Predicts P substitution for Al corner positions in E21 κ-carbide is energetically most favorable, reducing {100} cleavage stress by 45%.
45% reduction in cleavage stress; open-volume defects on {100} when stress applied.
Substitution for Al relaxes local bonding along <100>κ. SRO kinetics calculation suggests P ordering into κ-carbide corner positions may be an order of magnitude faster than Al.

prediction.computational_details.sample_si107_lp_53: Experimental APT and XRD results confirm predictions
120 MPa/wt.% Si for specimens aged at 530-C for 60 h
Derived from the work of Kato et al. Predicted increase in yield strength due to Si addition is in reasonable agreement with reported mechanical properties.
prediction.computational_details.sample_si092_hp_53: Fracture mode observations support predicted 45% reduction in cleavage stress
SRO kinetics of P and Al
Thermodynamic preference of P substitution for Al was not taken into account.
SRO of P into E21 corner positions may be an order of magnitude faster than Al

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The nominal composition Fe-30%Mn-9%Al-0.9%C is nearly 15% less dense than quenched and tempered SAE 4130 steels.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Silicon additions decrease the melting point by 30°C/wt.% Si and increase fluidity for complex shape casting.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Silicon has been reported to suppress formation of brittle β-Mn during extended age hardening.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C (0.023% P) as-hot-rolled CVN energy was only 23 J (L–T) and 22 J (T–L), transitioning to 160 J after 1100°C solution treatment and water quench.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The mechanism of brittle cleavage-like fracture with aging is slipping off / glide plane decohesion along {111} slip planes, facilitated by planar slip and shear strain localization from κ-carbide precipitation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Figure 1a: as-cast Fe-28Mn-3Al-0.9Si-1.2C-0.6Mo steel microstructure of large primary austenite dendrites. Figure 1b: hot-rolled Fe-29Mn-6Al-0.9C-0.29Si-0.44Nb-0.44Mo equiaxed austenite grains, average grain size 50 μm, pinned by NbC on grain boundaries.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Figure 5: APT carbon concentration maps (2–6 at.% C) showing progress of spinodal decomposition in 0.006% P and 0.043% P steels aged 10 h at 530°C. Scale is in nanometers.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: A key conclusion is that further improvement requires grain-refining techniques or cast preforms for subsequent forging. Development of dispersoids to interfere with planar slip from SRO or κ-carbide shearing is needed to improve notch toughness and fatigue.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Misch metal (Ce and La) treatment combined with Ca and Ar stirring reduced phosphorus embrittlement effects and improved CVN impact energy from 33 J to 108 J in aged Fe-30Mn-9Al-1Si-0.9C-0.3Mo.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Figure 2a (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for low-P Fe-30Mn-9Al-XSi-0.9C steels: at 1 h, 0.59% Si = 210 BHN, 1.07% Si = 220 BHN, 1.56% Si = 215 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 280, 295 BHN; at 100 h, 340, 350, 345 BHN.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 2b (scatter plot embedded in text) gives 530-C age-hardening hardness values for 1% Si steels with varying P: at 1 h, 0.001% P = 215 BHN, 0.006% P = 225 BHN, 0.018% P = 275 BHN, 0.043% P = 245 BHN; at 10 h, 260, 275, 360, 340 BHN; at 100 h, 325, 345, 380, 370 BHN.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Table II contains additional tensile data for non-calcium-treated steels with higher P (0.06% P): 1.0Si ST UTS 687 MPa EL 44%; 1.4Si ST UTS 766 MPa EL 36%; 1.0Si aged 530-C 10h UTS 940 MPa EL 18%; 1.4Si aged 10h UTS 995 MPa EL 14%; 1.0Si aged 30h UTS 1065 MPa EL 8%; 1.4Si aged 30h UTS 1085 MPa EL 5%. A calcium-treated 0.78Si 0.007P as-cast alloy had YS 616 MPa, UTS 698 MPa, EL 17%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Figure 7 scatter data indicate wrought Fe-30Mn-9Al-1.01Si-0.9C-0.5Mo UTS values of approximately 950 MPa (as-solution treated), 1050 MPa (2h aged), 1070 MPa (10h), and 1160 MPa (50h), with corresponding cast values of 760 MPa (20h) and 1000 MPa (50h). Wrought elongation ranged from about 15% at 1160 MPa UTS to 55% at 950 MPa UTS, while cast elongation was about 18% at 1000 MPa and 36% at 770 MPa.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Figure 11 line/scatter data for CVN versus temperature: 1.07% Si 12h aged at 530-C gives energies from 42 J at -40-C to 92 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 12h aged gives 21 J at -40-C to 68 J at 25-C; 1.07% Si 30h aged gives 20 J at -40-C to 58 J at 25-C; 1.56% Si 30h aged gives 12 J at -40-C to 33 J at 25-C.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Hot-rolled Fe-30Mn-9Al-2.7Si-0.9C with 0.023% P had CVN breaking energy of only 23 J (L-T) and 22 J (T-L) in the as-hot-rolled condition, improving to 160 J (L-T) after solution treatment for 10 min at 1100-C and water quenching.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The density reduction of these lightweight steels is up to 15% compared with conventional quenched and tempered steels and 17% reduction potential is mentioned for Al contents between 4 and 12 weight percent.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Inclusion analysis (Fig. 15) shows total inclusion density similar between low-P Si-containing steels (62-78 inclusions/mm2), with the major difference being porosity: 0.59% Si steel had more than twice the porosity of 1.07% Si steel and almost double that of 1.56% Si steel.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Solution-treated cast steels with low phosphorus exhibit room-temperature Charpy V-notch impact energies up to 200 J and dynamic fracture toughness over 700 kJ/m2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Phosphorus in levels greater than 0.06% P greatly increases the kinetics of age hardening but sharply decreases CVN impact toughness by as much as 80%. AlN inclusions reduced CVN breaking energy at -40-C by almost 50%.
